task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
task.title: SAM : The 'License Update' Procedure

abstract.p: The 'License Update' Procedure is used to install licenses.

step.cmd: Plug the SAM key in a free USB port of your laptop (CodeMeter Control Center will automatically detect the SAM key and the color of the CodeMeter Control Center icon will change).
Launch CodeMeter Control Center (via 'start > Programs > CodeMeter >CodeMeter Control Center') or via the icon on the task bar:
Click on the “license update” button in the main screen of CodeMeter Control Center:
You now will get the window:
Click next and on the next screen select “Import license update”:
Click next and browse to the location where you stored the license file (which you received via mail) and click “Commit”:

info.p: The license is now added to the licenses already on the SAM key. When ready, you will get a message that the license update has been successfully imported.
Click OK and next finish. Now you will return to the main window of CodeMeter Control Center.

note.p: CodeMeter Control Center might now display no SAM key information. Activating a license and refreshing the information takes some time. You can speed this up by selecting 'View > Refresh'. If this doesn't result in an update, unplug and plug the SAM key again or stop and start the Code Meter Service (via 'Process > Stop Code Meter Service' and 'Process > Start Code Meter Service').
